events.text: Patient is a 67-year-old White female with a BMI of 29.4. Former smoker (15 pack-years, quit 20 years ago). No family history of breast or ovarian cancer.
At age 65, patient presented with a fungating left breast mass. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), breast NOS (C50.9), grade 3, ER negative, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 80%.
At age 65, left breast punch biopsy of fungating mass: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3. ER negative (

At age 65, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed a large left breast mass (8.5 cm) with extensive axillary, supraclavicular, and mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules and hepatic metastases.
At age 65, bone scan showed multifocal osseous metastases involving the spine, pelvis, and bilateral femora.
At age 65, MRI brain was negative for intracranial metastases.
At age 65, de novo stage IV (cT4bN3cM1) HER2-positive, ER/PR-negative inflammatory-type breast cancer with lung, liver, and bone metastases. Extensive disease burden.
At age 65, FoundationOne CDx: TP53 R273H, ERBB2 amplification (copy number 22), PIK3CA wild-type, PTEN loss. TMB 8 mutations/Mb. MSS.
At age 65, started first-line THP (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) with denosumab 120 mg monthly for bone metastases.
At age 65, baseline LVEF 55% on echocardiogram. Noted mild diastolic dysfunction attributed to hypertension.
At age 66, after cycle 2 of THP developed grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy. Grade 1 elevated AST.
At age 66, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed mixed response: liver lesions decreased but new pulmonary nodules identified. Overall stable disease.
At age 66, continued THP through 6 cycles. Docetaxel discontinued; maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab continued.
At age 66, LVEF declined to 48% on surveillance echocardiogram. Trastuzumab held for 1 cycle. Started lisinopril and carvedilol. Repeat echo showed recovery to 52%. Resumed HER2-directed therapy.
At age 67, PET/CT showed progressive disease with new liver lesions and increasing osseous disease burden.
At age 67, transitioned to second-line T-DM1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
At age 67, after 3 cycles of T-DM1, developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia (platelets 72,000) and grade 1 elevated transaminases. Dose reduced to 3.0 mg/kg.
At age 67, restaging CT after 4 cycles of T-DM1 showed stable disease. No new lesions.
At age 67, patient reports moderate fatigue limiting some daily activities. ECOG 2. Weight stable. Plan to continue T-DM1 with dose reduction. Discussed goals of care and palliative care referral.
At age 67, grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea on T-DM1. Peripheral neuropathy stable at grade 1.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
